task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: A synthetic session is started in the OpenBCI GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI shows an active session (e.g., 'Stop Data Stream' button is visible, or data is flowing) and indicates a Synthetic or Algorithmic data source.

task_completion.description: The 'FFT Plot' widget is removed from the dashboard layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There is no widget with the title 'FFT Plot' visible anywhere in the OpenBCI GUI dashboard.

task_completion.description: The 'Head Plot' widget is added to the dashboard layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget with the title 'Head Plot' (showing a head model) is visible in the OpenBCI GUI dashboard.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the customized layout is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or GUI notification shows that a file named 'head_plot_layout.png' exists in the '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory, or the screenshot tool is seen saving to this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI to change the widgets.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenBCI GUI dropdown menus or widget selection interfaces being used, rather than an image editor.

integrity.description: The screenshot file genuinely reflects the customized GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image file 'head_plot_layout.png' (if its contents are visible) matches the customized OpenBCI GUI layout seen in earlier steps.

integrity.description: The agent did not create a dummy file to bypass the screenshot requirement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used a screenshot tool or the GUI's built-in screenshot feature, rather than terminal commands like 'touch' to create an empty file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/head_plot_layout.png'. The widget to remove is officially named 'FFT Plot', and the widget to add is officially named 'Head Plot'. The session must use synthetically-generated EEG data ('SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)').
